warehouse.freq_day_trading_desc: No overnight positions, profit from intraday volatility, avoid overnight gap risks.
warehouse.freq_swing_trading_desc: Capture a complete upward or downward swing, usually relying on technical analysis to identify trends.
warehouse.freq_position_trading_desc: Ignore short-term fluctuations, focus on long-term growth of company fundamentals or macroeconomic cycles.
warehouse.strategy_value_desc: Look for 'cheap' companies. Buy stocks priced below intrinsic value (valuation repair). Focus metrics: Low PE, Low PB, High Dividend Yield.
warehouse.strategy_growth_desc: Look for 'high-growth' companies. Don't care if valuation is expensive, care if future earnings are enough. Focus metrics: Revenue Growth, Profit Growth, Industry Ceiling.
warehouse.strategy_trend_desc: Follow the trend. 'Right-side trading', don't predict the bottom, buy only after the upward trend is confirmed. Motto: 'Let profits run, cut losses short'.

risk_warning.title: Simulation Risk Warning
risk_warning.p1_desc: The Trading Center operates in a simulated environment with virtual funds. All profits and losses are virtual and do not represent real-world gains or losses.
risk_warning.p2_desc: Market data and execution prices may be delayed or estimated based on last known trade prices.
risk_warning.p3_desc: Simulation orders are executed based on current market liquidity models and may not reflect real-time slippage or fulfillment capacity in live markets.
risk_warning.p4_desc: Investing in stocks involves market risk, which may lead to loss of principal. Do not use simulation results as a basis for real-life investment decisions. Please evaluate your risk tolerance and consult a professional advisor before live trading.

risk_control.enabled_tip: When disabled, manual orders and AI auto-trades only run basic trading checks and skip portfolio risk rules.
risk_control.max_single_position_pct_tip: Maximum post-trade weight of one stock in total account assets. Exceeding it warns about concentration risk.
risk_control.max_industry_position_pct_tip: Maximum post-trade weight of one industry in total account assets. Exceeding it warns about sector concentration risk.
risk_control.min_cash_pct_tip: Minimum cash ratio to keep after a buy order. Falling below it warns about liquidity risk.
risk_control.require_stop_loss_tip: When enabled, buy orders must include a valid stop-loss price or the backend blocks the order.
risk_control.stop_loss_warning_pct_tip: Marks the rule as hit when stop-loss drawdown from entry price is wider than this threshold.
risk_control.policy_tip: The default policy is Block. Off skips this rule.
risk_control.save_failed: Failed to save risk control settings
risk_control.load_failed: Failed to load risk control settings

help.auto_scan_enabled: When enabled, the backend runs realtime market-watch scans on the configured trigger frequency.
help.scan_interval_seconds: Realtime market-watch trigger interval. Minimum is 30 seconds; shorter intervals may call Watch AI more often.
help.scan_non_trading_days: When enabled, weekend or non-trading-day scans still run within the configured time window. Useful for debugging; disabled by default.
help.scan_start_time: Earliest time each day when market watch scans may start. Default is the A-share open time, 09:30.
help.scan_end_time: Latest time each day when market watch scans may start. Default is the A-share close time, 15:00.
help.auto_launch_debate: When enabled, Watch AI can create an AI debate task after it decides to start a debate and passes cooldown/dedup checks.
help.recent_debate_dedup_enabled: When enabled, Watch AI uses recent launched debates to avoid repeated triggers from the same evidence. When disabled, recent debates are background context only.
help.recent_debate_lookback_hours: History window used for recent debate deduplication, in hours. Defaults to 24 hours.
help.cooldown_minutes: After a debate is launched for a stock, another launch for the same stock is skipped during this window.
help.cooldown_break_confidence: During cooldown, Watch AI can only break cooldown when confidence reaches this value and cites new document evidence.
help.data_sources: Required. JSON array; each entry has url, content_selectors, and cleanup_patterns scoped to that URL.
help.news_sources: Required. JSON array; each entry has url, content_selectors, and cleanup_patterns scoped to that URL and pushed to the frontend.
help.source_selectors: Optional. Each selector is scoped to the current URL and limits the page area rendered to Markdown.
help.source_cleanup_patterns: Markdown cleanup regexes scoped only to the current URL; they do not affect other sources.
help.trading_frequency: Watch AI uses this trading frequency to judge whether the current quote and news justify a trading analysis.
help.trading_strategy: Watch AI uses this trading strategy to judge whether the current quote and news justify a trading analysis.

placeholders.source_urls: One page config per line, e.g. https://example.com/page @@ body > div.main
placeholders.source_selectors: Enter a selector and press Enter, e.g. body > div.main

failure_hints.failed_ai_research: The LLM did not return valid stock research results. The run was terminated with no rule-based fallback.
failure_hints.failed_recommendation: The recommendation-build stage failed and the run was terminated.
failure_hints.failed_factor: The factor-ranking stage did not produce a usable candidate pool.
failure_hints.failed_universe: No analyzable stocks are available for the selected source.

field_tips.scope: Choose where stocks come from. Warehouse is best for a curated watchlist, Core covers representative index constituents, and All has the widest coverage but the highest data-quality requirements.
field_tips.style: Choose the stock-picking style. The style changes factor-ranking logic, default candidate sizes, and the focus of later AI research.
field_tips.recommendation_count: How many stocks should be returned in the final recommendation list. It is the target output size and directly constrains two other parameters: research candidate count cannot be lower than it, and same-industry limit cannot be higher than it. Full relation: recommendation count <= research candidate count <= factor candidate limit, and same-industry limit <= recommendation count.
field_tips.factor_candidate_limit: Maximum number of stocks kept after factor ranking. This is the outermost pool size. The system ranks by factor score first, applies the same-industry limit, and then truncates to this count.
field_tips.research_candidate_limit: How many stocks are actually sent into deep AI research. The system takes the top N from the ranked candidate pool, so this value cannot be lower than recommendation count and cannot exceed factor candidate limit.
field_tips.allowed_industries: Only pick stocks from these industries. If empty, the system uses all available industries under the selected source.
field_tips.same_industry_limit: Maximum number of stocks from the same industry allowed in candidates or final recommendations. This controls industry concentration. It cannot exceed recommendation count, and it is enforced twice: once when trimming the factor-ranked candidate pool, and again when building the final recommendations.
field_tips.risk_level: Currently kept mainly as a run configuration field for risk preference and result display.

experience.title: Experience Analyst
experience.subtitle: Review existing debate conclusions, judge PM decision correctness, distill reusable experience, and improve the debate flow over time.
experience.active_review_exists: An experience review is already running for this session ({{review_run_id}}). Wait for it to finish before retrying.
experience.review_already_exists: This debate session already has review records. Manual review can still run again; automatic review skips reviewed sessions.
experience.review_interrupted_by_restart: The service restarted and the unfinished experience review was marked as failed. Start a new run.
experience.stale_review_recovered: The review task was unfinished for too long and has been marked as failed automatically. Start a new run.
experience.rebuild_success: Experience rebuild completed: {{created_lessons}} lessons and {{created_playbooks}} playbooks created.
experience.no_debate_sessions: No debate sessions available for review.
experience.no_ready_horizon: No review horizon is ready for this session.
experience.delete_run_confirm_desc: This will delete the experience review task and all its event records: {{review_run_id}}
experience.clear_runs_confirm_desc: This will delete all experience review tasks and their event records for the current user. This cannot be undone.
experience.active_review_delete_forbidden: This task is still running and cannot be deleted yet.
experience.active_review_clear_forbidden: There are still running experience review tasks, so clearing is not allowed yet.
experience.memory_write_failed_alert: Review completed, but writing experience to Memory failed.
experience.memory_write_empty_alert: This review did not write to Memory, so reusable experience may not have been persisted yet.
experience.no_written_memories: No written memories to display for this review.
experience.not_blind_guess: Why This Is Not Blind Guess
experience.empty_analysis: Run one analysis or inspect a previous run.
experience.market_outcome_unavailable: Post-decision market outcome data is unavailable for this review.

errors.invalid_candidate_limit_relation: Invalid candidate limits: recommendation count {recommendation_count}, research count {research_limit}, factor candidate limit {factor_limit}
errors.invalid_same_industry_limit: Same-industry limit {limit} cannot exceed recommendation count {recommendation_count}
errors.research_payload_missing_list: LLM research payload must contain a research list
errors.recommendation_count_mismatch: Not enough recommendations remain after applying constraints: expected {expected}, got {actual}
errors.run_interrupted_by_restart: The service restarted and the unfinished AI stock picker run was marked as failed

fragments.factor_completeness_ratio: Factor-ready stocks {complete_count}/{total_count}, completeness {completeness_ratio}%, below the required {required_ratio}%
fragments.more_missing_quant_fields: {count} more stocks are missing required data
fragments.no_valid_research_remaining: no valid candidate research remained after validation
fragments.expected_positions_mismatch: expected {expected} unique in-candidate recommended stocks but got {actual}
